definition: NAIRU point estimates carry confidence intervals (Staiger-Stock-Watson 95% CI ~4.3-7.3%) so wide as to span the entire policy-relevant range, making real-time NAIRU-based analysis functionally useless for policy or portfolio positioning. CBO retrospective revisions to NAIRU have exceeded the deviations that triggered policy responses.
evidence: Staiger-Stock-Watson 95% CI of ~4.3-7.3% spans the entire policy-relevant range
CBO retrospective revisions to NAIRU larger than deviations that triggered policy responses
Both sides of pair_3 debate assigned 9/10, no agent challenged the underlying econometrics
With unemployment at ~4.0-4.2%, cannot determine in real time whether economy is above, at, or below the natural rate
open_questions: Can NAIRU be usefully estimated using higher-frequency or alternative data sources?
Should labor market analysis abandon NAIRU entirely or use it as a wide-band regime indicator?
